supplyMechanism.mintCause: A PoT verdict verified===1 for one specific process is the ONLY cause of a mint (I1).
supplyMechanism.burnRule: The process part minted for a process is burned atomically on completion of that same process (I2); net process supply change per cycle = 0.
supplyMechanism.lastingSupply: Only the earned/retained commission persists (I3); totalSupply converges to earnedRetained.

commission.charged: A × rate on each confirmed process (the earned part, I3)

splitDestinations.nodePayment: SYSTEM_NODE_POOL — payment to nodes for confirmed work (I3), retained
splitDestinations.reserveAccrual: SYSTEM_RESERVE — AST's own reserve (I4), recorded as reserve.accrual

governance.issuer: AST Core Protocol (no privileged issuer of units; only PoT verdicts cause mints — I1)
governance.model: role-based committee, decisions recorded in NodeChain before effect (I8)

invariants.I1: PoT-gated origin — a unit exists only as the consequence of a confirmed process.
invariants.I2: Born-and-burned — the process part is minted then burned atomically; net-zero per cycle.
invariants.I3: Payment for confirmed work — nodes are paid post-factum for confirmed work; the earned part is retained.
invariants.I4: Reserve is AST's own — reserve share accrues to AST; index derived from confirmed volume only.
invariants.I5: Determinism — every movement is reproducible from canonical inputs in NodeChain.
invariants.I6: No speculative surface — no cap, no held speculation, no staking-for-yield, no governance-by-holding, no volatility control.
invariants.I7: All-Seeing Eye — observes and can veto; never initiates a mint, burn, or payment.
invariants.I8: Append-only causality — every cause is recorded before its effect is acknowledged.

compatibility.note: No external chain, wallet-bridge, or cross-chain wrapper. ARO exists only within AST (I1, I6).

security.killSwitch: read-only halt; in-flight process parts resolved to satisfy I2; engaged by Eye veto or role committee, never a single authority

metadata.supersedes: 1.0.0 (removed the non-canonical external reserve split, the governance-triggered burn, and external bridgeability as canon violations)
